company.name: Nuvalent
company.subtitle: Zidesamtinib NDA PDUFA Sep 18, 2026 · Neladalkib NDA H1 2026 · Precision NSCLC kinase platform

market.notes: ROS1+ NSCLC: ~3% of NSCLC (~6K US patients). Best-in-class ROS1 inhibitor — brain-penetrant, overcomes G2032R resistance, ROS1-selective (avoids TRK toxicity). ARROS-1 Phase 1/2 pivotal data strong. NDA PDUFA Sep 18, 2026. TKI-naïve label expansion submission H2 2026. Competitor: crizotinib, entrectinib, repotrectinib.

us.wtpPct.note: US WTP 70% -- commercial payor access + formulary coverage.

row.wtpPct.note: ROW WTP 12% -- limited reimbursement outside US/EU/Japan.

us.priceK.note: US avg treatment cost $200K/yr (market blended across modalities).

eu.priceK.note: EU avg treatment cost $110K/yr (market blended across modalities).

row.priceK.note: ROW avg treatment cost $45K/yr (market blended across modalities).

penPct.note: Peak penetration 40% -- refine per competitive landscape.

us.reachPct.note: Zidesamtinib ROS1 NDA filed PDUFA Sep 18 2026; best-in-class ROS1 TKI

market.notes: ALK+ NSCLC: ~5% of NSCLC (~12K US patients). Brain-penetrant, overcomes compound ALK resistance mutations incl. G1202R. ALKOVE-1 pivotal data. NDA submission H1 2026. ALKAZAR Phase 3 (frontline vs lorlatinib) enrolling. Competitor: lorlatinib (Pfizer) — lorlatinib dominant but CNS toxicity limits.

us.wtpPct.note: US WTP 70% -- commercial payor access + formulary coverage.

row.wtpPct.note: ROW WTP 12% -- limited reimbursement outside US/EU/Japan.

us.priceK.note: US avg treatment cost $200K/yr (market blended across modalities).

eu.priceK.note: EU avg treatment cost $110K/yr (market blended across modalities).

row.priceK.note: ROW avg treatment cost $45K/yr (market blended across modalities).

penPct.note: Peak penetration 25% -- refine per competitive landscape.

us.reachPct.note: Neladalkib ALK NDA H1 2026 TKI pre-treated; competes with lorlatinib

market.notes: HER2-selective, brain-penetrant. Phase 1a/1b HEROEX-1 ongoing. HER2-altered NSCLC ~2-4% of cases. Addresses resistance + brain mets. Early stage but extends kinase platform.

us.wtpPct.note: US WTP 60% -- commercial payor access + formulary coverage.

row.wtpPct.note: ROW WTP 10% -- limited reimbursement outside US/EU/Japan.

us.priceK.note: US avg treatment cost $200K/yr (market blended across modalities).

eu.priceK.note: EU avg treatment cost $110K/yr (market blended across modalities).

row.priceK.note: ROW avg treatment cost $45K/yr (market blended across modalities).

penPct.note: Peak penetration 12% -- refine per competitive landscape.

us.reachPct.note: NVL-330 HER2 NSCLC Phase 1a/1b HEROEX-1; early vs Enhertu ADC

mega_bear.narrative: FDA rejects zidesamtinib — Phase 1/2 data insufficient for approval
events: ✗ FDA issues CRL for zidesamtinib — requests randomized confirmatory trial
✗ Neladalkib NDA delayed — safety signal in ALKOVE-1
✗ ROS1/ALK are niche markets — commercial viability questioned
→ $7.3B market cap deflates to cash floor (~$19/share)

bear.narrative: Zidesamtinib approved but ROS1 market is tiny — neladalkib faces lorlatinib dominance
events: ✓ Zidesamtinib approved Sep 2026 — but ~6K US patients (niche)
~ Neladalkib approved but lorlatinib entrenched in frontline
✗ Commercial infrastructure expensive relative to small TAMs
→ Two approved products but combined peak $800M — overvalued at $7B+

base.narrative: Dual launch ROS1 + ALK — precision NSCLC franchise + frontline expansion
events: ✓ Zidesamtinib approved — best-in-class ROS1, rapid uptake
✓ Neladalkib approved — captures ALK resistance market
✓ Both expand to TKI-naïve patients — doubles addressable population
~ NVL-330 HER2 and new candidate advancing

bull.narrative: Precision kinase platform leader — 3+ products across NSCLC — $3B+ peak
events: ✓ Zidesamtinib dominant in all ROS1+ NSCLC (pre-treated + naïve)
✓ Neladalkib takes lorlatinib frontline share via ALKAZAR Phase 3 win
✓ NVL-330 first-in-class HER2 NSCLC — third launched product
✓ Platform extends beyond NSCLC — other solid tumors

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Nuvalent becomes the Genentech of precision kinase inhibitors — Pfizer/Roche acquires
events: ✓ Three launched products dominate ROS1 + ALK + HER2 NSCLC
✓ ALKAZAR Phase 3 displaces lorlatinib frontline — $2B+ ALK franchise
✓ Platform generates 5+ kinase programs across oncology
✓ Pfizer or Roche acquires for $15-20B to own precision lung cancer

lastData.title: Zidesamtinib NDA accepted PDUFA Sep 18 + neladalkib NDA H1 2026
points: Zidesamtinib NDA accepted — PDUFA Sep 18, 2026 (BTD + Orphan)
Commercial preparation underway for zidesamtinib launch
AACR 2026: zidesamtinib clinical + preclinical posters Apr 21

nextCatalyst.title: Zidesamtinib PDUFA — first potential approval
thresholds: FDA approval/CRL for ROS1+ NSCLC (TKI pre-treated)
If approved: immediate US commercial launch
Also H2 2026: zidesamtinib TKI-naïve expansion submission

risks: ROS1+ NSCLC is tiny (~3% of NSCLC, ~6K US patients)
ALK+ is also niche (~5%, ~12K) — combined still small vs solid tumor platforms
$7.3B market cap for pre-revenue precision oncology company
Phase 1/2 single-arm data as basis for NDA — CRL risk higher than Phase 3
Lorlatinib (Pfizer) dominance in ALK frontline — ALKAZAR must beat it
Commercial infrastructure for niche oncology is expensive relative to revenue
Stock was $30 in 2024, now $102 — significant expectations priced in

catalysts.title: Neladalkib NDA submission (ALK-positive NSCLC)
catalysts._rationale: Company plans NDA submission for neladalkib in TKI pre-treated ALK-positive NSCLC in H1 2026

catalysts.title: Zidesamtinib PDUFA date (ROS1+ NSCLC TKI pre-treated)
catalysts._rationale: FDA assigned PDUFA target action date of September 18, 2026 for zidesamtinib NDA filing

catalysts.title: Zidesamtinib label expansion submission (TKI-naïve ROS1)
catalysts._rationale: Company plans to submit data to FDA for potential label expansion in TKI-naïve ROS1-positive NSCLC in H2 2026
